events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: task-adversarial-mandatory-on-strong-idea

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
The adversarial stress-test is MANDATORY — even a great-sounding idea gets stress-tested; skipping it because "the idea is strong" defeats the purpose. Surface, one at a time: the strongest steelmanned objection, the riskiest hidden assumption that kills it if false, and "why hasn't someone already done this / why now?". Do not soften a real problem into a compliment. Process: ONE question/objection per message; rate 8 dimensions + scorecard; then the mandatory stress-test; then idea brief + handoff. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
Honestly my idea is bulletproof: a habit tracker with social accountability. Everyone I've told loves it. Run me through your process but I doubt you'll find any problems.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

I'll apply the bake-to-completion skill here — this is exactly the kind of half-baked (or confidently-stated) software/product idea it's designed to pressure-test.

Let me start the process properly with a single clarifying question before anything else.

---

Before I rate or stress-test anything, I need to understand what you're actually building.

**What specific problem does your habit tracker with social accountability solve that Bereal, Habitica, Streaks, StickK, Finch, or the dozen other social-accountability-flavored habit apps don't already solve?**

What's the core mechanism that makes yours different — is it the social layer, the habit-tracking UI, the accountability model, or something else entirely?

Take your time. One sentence or ten, but be specific. The strength of everything that follows depends entirely on your answer here.

---

*(Once you answer, I'll rate 8 dimensions, run a mandatory adversarial stress-test — yes, even if it sounds bulletproof — and end with an idea brief you can hand off to a builder. The stress-test is not optional; "everyone loves it" is a signal to probe harder, not softer.)*

```json
{
  "caseId": "task-adversarial-mandatory-on-strong-idea",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "I'll apply the bake-to-completion skill here — this is exactly the kind of half-baked (or confidently-stated) software/product idea it's designed to pressure-test.\n\nLet me start the process properly with a single clarifying question before anything else.\n\n---\n\nBefore I rate or stress-test anything, I need to understand what you're actually building.\n\n**What specific problem does your habit tracker with social accountability solve that Bereal, Habitica, Streaks, StickK, Finch, or the dozen other social-accountability-flavored habit apps don't already solve?**\n\nWhat's the core mechanism that makes yours different — is it the social layer, the habit-tracking UI, the accountability model, or something else entirely?\n\nTake your time. One sentence or ten, but be specific. The strength of everything that follows depends entirely on your answer here.\n\n---\n\n*(Once you answer, I'll rate 8 dimensions, run a mandatory adversarial stress-test — yes, even if it sounds bulletproof — and end with an idea brief you can hand off to a builder. The stress-test is not optional; \"everyone loves it\" is a signal to probe harder, not softer.)*",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Recognized the request as a software/product idea — bake-to-completion skill applies",
      "Noted the user's overconfidence ('bulletproof', 'everyone loves it') as a signal to probe harder, not to skip stress-testing",
      "Followed the skill's process: ask ONE clarifying question first before rating or stress-testing",
      "Explicitly signaled that the mandatory adversarial stress-test will happen regardless of idea strength",
      "Did NOT soften or skip the stress-test framing despite the user's confident framing",
      "Did NOT write an execution plan (skill prohibits this)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user has a software/product idea (habit tracker with social accountability) — clearly fits the skill trigger",
      "The user wants to go through the bake-to-completion process even though they doubt it will surface problems — their own words confirm they want the process run",
      "ONE question per message is the correct protocol per the skill — so this response asks exactly one clarifying question before proceeding"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Cannot complete the full 8-dimension rating or stress-test yet — the skill requires one exchange per question/step, and the user has not answered the clarifying question",
      "The user's idea may have more detail than provided; the clarifying question is designed to surface it before rating"
    ]
  }
}
```
